comments.author: @World_Untold_321
comments.text: Papua New Guinea has always fascinated me with its incredibly rich culture and untouched landscapes. Watching how local communities maintain their traditional way of life in harmony with nature—despite the modern world changing so fast—is deeply inspiring. Truly an eye-opening documentary!

comments.author: @travelintolife
comments.text: The farther a place is from the modern world, the more it reminds me how many ways there are to live a meaningful life. Papua New Guinea is truly eye-opening.

comments.author: @emilylopili-kakpo2181
comments.text: I am a Papua New Guinean. You made me proud of who I truly represent....is my home. We own land passed down through generations both in matrilineal and patrilineal systems. Great coverage and brings positivity to the international viewers. There is so much more to PNG if you visit the provinces.

comments.text: Watching from Nigeria and the time is 8:08 PM

comments.author: @RealLifeFilesDoc
comments.text: The anthropological depth of this documentary is mind-blowing. The fact that the entire Highlands population existed completely undiscovered until 1930 [13:05]—while the rest of the world was driving cars and watching movies—is surreal. I also love how the Goroka Show was engineered by administrators in 1957 to replace tribal warfare with cultural competition [15:17]. Using performance to build peace is an incredible piece of human history. Halo from Vietnam! 👏

comments.author: @XGlobeExplorer-JQK
comments.text: The fact that the Goroka Show started as a way to replace warfare with cultural celebration is so powerful. It’s not just a festival; it’s 700 nations honoring their ancestors together.
